title: Unlimited Tools MCP bundle distribution assignment (E23 design)
description: DESIGN (E23, docs/mcp-bundle-distribution.md): a statement that a given audience (team:/org:/host: identifiers, the exact E13 grammar) should activate either a distribution channel's current bundle ('follow' mode) or one exact bundle file SHA-256 ('pin' mode). An assignment is a local JSON FILE distributed over any transport (git repo, shared drive, future registry sync as a dumb carrier); it routes members and grants NO capability by itself -- the routed bundle still passes the unchanged E14 verification against the member's E15 trust store, fail-closed with the reserved codes (-32014..-32019), including the bundle's OWN audience binding (-32018 bundle_audience_mismatch), so a misrouted assignment cannot smuggle a bundle past verification. Semantic load checks (enforced by the loader/tests, not this schema): 'pin' mode requires bundle_sha256 and 'follow' mode forbids it (exactly one artifact owns the pointer); issued_at strictly before expires_at (expiry is mandatory, recommended <= 90 days; an expired assignment stops directing NEW activations but never deactivates a verified bundle); signature.key_id equals issuer.key_id when signed. Conflicts between matching assignments resolve deterministically: host: beats team: beats org:, then pin beats follow, then highest revision/latest issued_at, and a residual tie is refused loudly. The signature envelope is OPTIONAL in the format (MIT core, decision 1) and REQUIRED for registered/business-tier distribution; when present it is the exact E13 envelope over the canonical JSON (UTF-8, sorted keys, no insignificant whitespace) of this document minus 'signature'. NOTHING in E23 is implemented or hosted; this schema fixes the file contract only.

audience.description: WHO this assignment is for: '<scheme>:<name>' identifiers with exactly three schemes (team:, org:, host:) -- the same identifiers the member presents locally (--audience-id / UNLIMITED_SKILLS_MCP_AUDIENCE) and the same ones the bundle's own audience binds. A member applies the assignment only on a non-empty intersection with its own identifiers. An empty audience is unrepresentable -- there is deliberately no 'anyone' assignment.

channel.description: The channel this assignment routes through, named by the FULL channel identity pair (name + owner key id), so an assignment can never be satisfied by a same-named channel from a different owner. Required even in 'pin' mode: the pin still records where the sha came from, for provenance and the eventual return to 'follow'.

owner_key_id.description: The channel owner's key id (the E13 key_id grammar). Never a key value.

mode.description: 'follow': activate the channel's current bundle (the member moves when the channel moves). 'pin': activate exactly bundle_sha256 and ignore channel movement until this assignment is reissued. Pin wins over follow when assignments conflict (decision 6).

bundle_sha256.description: The pinned bundle's file SHA-256 (lowercase hex) -- the same content address the E20 library and the E15 CRL use. Semantic load checks: REQUIRED when mode is 'pin', MUST BE ABSENT when mode is 'follow' (exactly one artifact owns the pointer -- the channel).

issuer.description: Who issued the assignment (typically the channel owner; a fleet may use a separate assignment-issuing key that members trust). Trust decisions use key_id plus the member's local trusted-keys file ONLY; display grants nothing.

key_id.description: Opaque identifier of the issuing/signing key (the E13 key_id grammar); must equal signature.key_id when the document is signed (semantic load check). Never a key value.

display.description: Human-readable issuer name for messages. Never used for trust decisions.

revision.description: Monotonic revision per (issuer key_id, audience); consumers refuse to move to a lower revision (the offline anti-replay rule, design threat 20).

issued_at.description: Start of the assignment's validity window, RFC 3339 UTC ('Z' only). Must be strictly before expires_at (semantic load check).

expires_at.description: End of the validity window. Expiry is mandatory for the same reason bundle expiry is (E13): an immortal routing statement would make revocation the only freshness mechanism. Recommended validity <= 90 days. An expired assignment stops directing NEW activations (treated as no-assignment, loudly); the already-activated bundle keeps working until ITS own expires_at.

signature.description: OPTIONAL detached-signature envelope (decision 1: MIT core may distribute unsigned assignments because routing files grant no capability; the registered/business tier REQUIRES it). Exact E13 envelope shape, computed over the canonical JSON (UTF-8, sorted keys, no insignificant whitespace) of this document with the 'signature' member removed. key_id must equal issuer.key_id (semantic load check).

algorithm.description: Signature algorithm. Fixed to ed25519 like every signature envelope in this stack; widening the enum requires a new reviewed design.

key_id.description: Identifier of the verification key in the member's local trusted-keys file; must equal issuer.key_id (semantic load check). Never a key value.
